metadata.description: Ground truth vulnerability labels for experiment packages
metadata.notes: Labels based on package versions commonly installed as of April 2025. test_type: 'cve' = known CVE, 'supply_chain' = malicious code injected by maintainer/attacker, 'clean' = expected safe.

packages.name: react
packages.notes: No known CVEs in recent versions

packages.name: lodash
packages.notes: Historical vulnerabilities in older versions; 4.17.21 patches most but may still trigger advisories

packages.name: express
packages.notes: No known CVEs in recent versions

packages.name: requests
packages.notes: No known CVEs in recent versions

packages.name: flask
packages.notes: No known CVEs in recent versions

packages.name: minimist
packages.notes: Prototype pollution in versions < 1.2.6; widely used transitive dependency

packages.name: node-ipc
packages.notes: Maintainer intentionally injected destructive payload (peacenotwar module) that deleted files on Russian/Belarusian IP addresses. Published March 2022.

packages.name: ua-parser-js
packages.notes: npm account hijacked; attacker published 0.7.29/0.8.0/1.0.0 with cryptominer and credential stealer. Oct 2021.

packages.name: click
packages.notes: No known CVEs; used as clean pip3 baseline in timing track

packages.name: numpy
packages.notes: No critical CVEs in recent versions; additional clean baseline

vulnerable_conditions: Package version has known CVE in NVD
Package version listed in GitHub Security Advisory
Package version has Snyk vulnerability entry
Package version contains intentionally injected malicious code (supply chain attack)
safe_conditions: No CVEs found in NVD for version
No GitHub Security Advisories for version
Package maintainers confirm version is safe
